session.user: Load and follow the skill instructions in .claude/skills/pr-feedback-classifier/SKILL.md Arguments: --pr 8884 Return the complete JSON output as your final message.
session.assistant: I'll load the pr-feedback-classifier skill and run it with the specified arguments.
session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→name: pr-feedback-classifier 3→description: > 4→ Fetches and classifies PR review feedback with context isolation. 5→ Returns structured JSON with thread IDs for deterministic resolution. 6→ Use when analyzing PR comments before addressing them. 7→argument-hint: "[--pr <number>] [--include-resolved]" 8→context: fork 9→agent: general-purpose 10→model: sonnet 11→--- 12→ 13→# PR Feedback Classifier 14→ 15→Fetch and classify all PR review feedback for the current branch's PR. 16→ 17→## Arguments 18→ 19→- `--pr <number>`: Target a specific PR by number (default: current branch's PR) 20→- `--include-resolved`: Include resolved threads (for reference) 21→ 22→Check `$ARGUMENTS` for flags. 23→ 24→## Critical Constraints 25→ 26→**DO NOT write Python scripts or any code files.** Classify the data using direct AI reasoning only. Writing code to process JSON is unnecessary and pollutes the filesystem. 27→ 28→## Steps 29→ 30→1. **Fetch PR info and all comments in a single call:**
... [194 lines omitted] ...
130→ "action_summary": "Bot suggestion: add unit tests for error handling paths", 132→ "original_comment": "Consider adding unit tests for the error handling paths in this endpoint" 170→ "error": null 184→## Error Case 198→ "error": "No PR found for branch feature-xyz" 216→ "error": null
session.assistant: Now I'll execute the skill by running the `erk exec get-pr-feedback` command with the specified PR number:

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: Get file restructuring context for current branch

session.tool_result:
{ "success": true, "pr_number": 8884, "pr_url": "https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/8884", "pr_title": "Fix `issue_number` references in docs/learned/", "review_threads": [ { "id": "PRRT_kwDOPxC3hc5yuEYl", "path": "docs/learned/architecture/derived-flags.md", "line": 28, "is_outdated": false, "comments": [ { "author": "github-actions", "body": "**Audit PR Docs**: Verbatim source code copy detected + inaccurate claim.

Source: `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/submit_pipeline.py:276-277`

This code block copies from the source file and is also incomplete — the actual source is:
```python
is_plan_impl = state.plan_id is not None or state.branch_name.startswith("plnd/")
```

The doc omits the `or state.branch_name.startswith("plnd/")` fallback (needed when cleanup already deleted `.erk/impl-context/` but the first submit attempt failed before pushing).

Suggested fix: Replace with a source pointer and note both conditions in prose:
> See `_run_phase1_graphite_submit()` in `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/submit_pipeline.py`. The condition combines `state.plan_id is not None` (normal case) with `state.branch_name.startswith("plnd/")` (fallback when impl-context was deleted before push).", "created_at": "2026-03-07T01:54:40Z" } ] }, { "id": "PRRT_kwDOPxC3hc5yuEbr", "path": "docs/learned/architecture/plan-context-integration.md", "line": 83, "is_outdated": false, "comments": [ { "author": "github-actions", "body": "**Audit PR Docs**: Verbatim source code copy detected.

Source: `src/erk/core/plan_context_provider.py:16-28`

This code block copies ~7 lines verbatim from the source file and will go stale if the source changes.

Suggested fix: Replace with the source pointer format from `docs/learned/documentation/source-pointers.md` (HTML comment + prose reference):
```
<!-- Source: src/erk/core/plan_context_provider.py, PlanContext -->
See `PlanContext` in `src/erk/core/plan_context_provider.py`. Key fields: `plan_id` (str), `plan_content` (str), `objective_summary` (str | None).
```", "created_at": "2026-03-07T01:54:49Z" }
... [69 lines omitted] ...
"body": "**Audit PR Docs**: Inaccurate claim detected.

Claim: "The pipeline detects plan implementations via `state.plan_id is not None`"
Reality: The actual source code at `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/submit_pipeline.py:276` is:
```python
is_plan_impl = state.plan_id is not None or state.branch_name.startswith("plnd/")
```

The doc omits the `or state.branch_name.startswith("plnd/")` fallback condition (handles the case when `.erk/impl-context/` cleanup occurs before a failed push attempt).

Source: `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/submit_pipeline.py:276`

Suggested fix: Update the prose description to mention both conditions, or replace with a source pointer: See `_run_phase1_graphite_submit()` in `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/submit_pipeline.py`.", "body": "**Audit PR Docs**: Inaccurate claim detected.

Claim: "plan implementations (`state.plan_id is not None`) auto-force and never hit this guard"
Reality: The actual source at `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/submit_pipeline.py:276` shows:
```python
is_plan_impl = state.plan_id is not None or state.branch_name.startswith("plnd/")
```

There is also a `plnd/` branch prefix fallback that triggers auto-force when `state.plan_id` is `None` (happens when `.erk/impl-context/` cleanup already occurred before a failed first push attempt).

Source: `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/submit_pipeline.py:273-277`

Suggested fix: Update to: "plan implementations (`state.plan_id is not None or state.branch_name.startswith('plnd/')`) auto-force..."", "body": "Plan Queued for Implementation

session.assistant: The git diff failed because we need to use the correct branch reference. Let me check the current branch setup:

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: git diff --stat -M -C origin/main...HEAD 2>/dev/null || git diff --stat -M -C HEAD~1...HEAD 2>/dev/null || git diff --stat -M -C
Get file restructuring context from current branch

session.assistant:
Now I have the PR feedback data. Let me classify the comments according to the skill's requirements. I'll analyze the review threads and discussion comments:
**Analysis:**
PR #8884 has 5 review threads, all from the `github-actions` bot. These are audit comments related to documentation issues. Let me classify them:
1. **derived-flags.md:28** - Verbatim code copy + inaccurate (missing fallback condition) 2. **plan-context-integration.md:83** (first comment) - Verbatim code copy (PlanContext dataclass) 3. **plan-context-integration.md:83** (second comment) - Inaccurate claim (pre-existing branch naming issue) 4. **pr-submit-pipeline.md:143** - Inaccurate claim (missing fallback condition) 5. **graphite-divergence-detection.md:35** - Inaccurate claim (missing fallback condition)
The git diff shows extensive restructuring with many file moves and changes, but this is a documentation PR, so we need to check for pre-existing detection carefully.
**Pre-Existing Detection:** - All comments are from `github-actions[bot]` (bot author) - The PR involves documentation changes across multiple files - Comments #4 and #5 flag the same incomplete condition issue (`is_plan_impl`) - Comment #3 flags a pre-existing branch naming convention that's outdated - Comments #1 and #2 are about verbatim code copies and accuracy
Based on the skill instructions, I'll classify:
```json { "success": true, "pr_number": 8884, "pr_title": "Fix `issue_number` references in docs/learned/", "pr_url": "https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/8884", "actionable_threads": [ { "thread_id": "PRRT_kwDOPxC3hc5yuEYl", "type": "review", "path": "docs/learned/architecture/derived-flags.md", "line": 28, "is_outdated": false, "classification": "actionable", "pre_existing": false, "action_summary": "Replace verbatim code copy with source pointer; include both conditions for is_plan_impl detection", "complexity": "single_file", "original_comment": "Verbatim source code copy detected + inaccurate claim. Doc omits the `or state.branch_name.startswith(\"plnd/\")` fallback." }, { "thread_id": "PRRT_kwDOPxC3hc5yuEbr", "type": "review", "path": "docs/learned/architecture/plan-context-integration.md", "line": 83, "is_outdated": false, "classification": "actionable", "pre_existing": false, "action_summary": "Replace verbatim PlanContext dataclass copy with source pointer format", "complexity": "single_file", "original_comment": "Verbatim source code copy detected from plan_context_provider.py. Replace with source pointer." }, { "thread_id": "PRRT_kwDOPxC3hc5yuEpY", "type": "review", "path": "docs/learned/architecture/plan-context-integration.md", "line": 83, "is_outdated": false, "classification": "actionable", "pre_existing": true, "action_summary": "Update branch naming convention table to reflect current plnd/ prefix pattern (pre-existing issue)", "complexity": "pre_existing", "original_comment": "Branch naming convention table shows old P-prefixed pattern; should show current plnd/ pattern" }, { "thread_id": "PRRT_kwDOPxC3hc5yuEsZ", "type": "review", "path": "docs/learned/cli/pr-submit-pipeline.md", "line": 143, "is_outdated": false, "classification": "actionable", "pre_existing": false, "action_summary": "Update prose to include both conditions for plan detection: state.plan_id is not None OR branch starts with plnd/", "complexity": "local", "original_comment": "Inaccurate claim.
